---
title: "1992-07 Dashboard"
subtitle: "Monthly Dashboard for Fabric Softener sold at Dominick's: July 1992"
draft: true
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-expand: 2

categories:
  - Dominicks
  - Fabric Softener
  - 1992-07
  - GEKS
---

# Overview

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
library(dplyr)
library(DT)
library(arrow)
library(plotly)

period_of_focus <- "1992-07"

ccdi_df <- read_parquet("../../../output/fsf_ccdi.parquet")

ccdi_df <- ccdi_df %>%
  arrange(period_of_focus) %>% # Ensure data is ordered chronologically first
  mutate(
    mom_ratio = score / lag(score, n = 1),
    yoy_ratio = score / lag(score, n = 12)
  )

# Extract the MoM ratio for the specific period
mom_value <- ccdi_df %>%
  filter(period_of_focus == .env$period) %>%
  pull(mom_ratio)

# Extract the YoY ratio for the specific period
yoy_value <- ccdi_df %>%
  filter(period_of_focus == .env$period) %>%
  pull(yoy_ratio)

# Create a small dataframe for the bar chart
bar_df <- data.frame(
  Metric = factor(c("YoY", "MoM"), levels = c("YoY", "MoM")), # YoY at bottom, MoM on top
  Value = c(yoy_value, mom_value)
)

ccdi_df_filtered <- ccdi_df %>%
  filter(period <= "1992-07")

fig <- plot_ly(ccdi_df_filtered, 
               x = ~period, 
               y = ~score, 
               type = 'scatter', 
               mode = 'lines',
               text = ~paste("Period:", period, "<br>Index:", score),
               hoverinfo = 'text') %>%
  layout(title = "CCDI with mean splice (Fabric Softeners category)",
         xaxis = list(title = "Time Period", type = 'category'), # Ensures order is maintained
         yaxis = list(title = "Index", tickformat = ".01"))


datatable(bar_df, options = list(
  dom = 't' # i.e. Table only
))
```


# Diagnostics

## Analytical (output) diagnostics


## Input diagnostics


## Monitoring / data validation

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-align: center
#| fig-height: 8
#| fig-width: 10
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

raw_data = read_parquet("data/processed/processed_fsf.parquet")
stop_at_period_data <- raw_data %>%
  filter(REF_PERIOD <= period_of_focus)

result <- stop_at_period_data %>%
  group_by(REF_PERIOD) %>%
  summarize(
    number_of_rows             = n(),                  # Count of rows
    total_sales        = sum(SALES),         # Sum of column2
    number_of_weeks    = n_distinct(WEEK),   # Unique count of column3
    start_of_month     = first(START),
    end_of_month       = last(END),
    distinct_stores    = n_distinct(STORE),
    distinct_upcs      = n_distinct(UPC),
    distinct_com_codes = n_distinct(COM_CODE),
    distinct_nitems    = n_distinct(NITEM),
    distinct_zones     = n_distinct(ZONE),
    .groups            = 'drop' # Drops the grouping structure after summarizing
  )

# Reshape the data to a long format, excluding date columns to avoid type conflicts
long_result <- result %>%
  select(-start_of_month, -end_of_month) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -REF_PERIOD, names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value")

# Create faceted boxplots and add a red dashed line for the period_of_focus
p <- ggplot(long_result, aes(x = Metric, y = Value)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "lightgray") +
  geom_hline(data = filter(long_result, REF_PERIOD == period_of_focus), 
             aes(yintercept = Value), color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Metric, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(), 
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(2, "lines") # Adds whitespace between the faceted panels
  ) +
  labs(title = paste("Key monitoring metrics, focus on", period_of_focus), y = "Value")

ggplotly(p)
```
